step_by_step_thinking:
I largely concur with the current assessment but would add another point of view based on practical experience. While preventing distortion remains important, washing with slurry also serves to improve surface texture compatibility, making sure there’s better adherence between the elastic material and subsequent layers like plaster or other casting materials. This improved adhesion can lead to reduced risk of leakage during pour-out processes and enhanced structural integrity post-setting.

raw_response:
I largely concur with the current assessment but would add another point of view based on practical experience. While preventing distortion remains important, washing with slurry also serves to improve surface texture compatibility, making sure there’s better adherence between the elastic material and subsequent layers like plaster or other casting materials. This improved adhesion can lead to reduced risk of leakage during pour-out processes and enhanced structural integrity post-setting.

Therefore, my agreement stands firm, reinforcing option C as the best fit given these additional insights. Answer choice: C. Agree with added insight about adhesive properties improvement through slurry use.
